Airfares Are Set to Rise Again as the Fuel Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Prices for flights will shoot up again if fuel costs stay high, the chief of British Airways has warned. headlines.title: Russia Slashes Oil Exports As Fuel Shortages And Drone Attacks Bite
headlines.summary: Russia is preparing to sharply reduce crude oil exports this month as mounting refinery disruptions, fuel shortages, and Ukraine’s bombing campaign force Moscow to divert more barrels into the domestic market. headlines.title: Aker BP Gains 2.2 Million Barrels With Tiny Stake Increase
headlines.summary: Aker BP will receive an additional 2.2 million barrels of oil equivalent after the partners in the Johan Sverdrup field completed a redetermination process that slightly increased the company’s ownership stake. The adjustment raises Aker BP’s interest in Johan Sverdrup to 31.7163

headlines.title: Weak Chinese Demand Forces Cuts to Iranian Crude Prices

headlines.title: The Countdown to a Major Oil Price Surge Has Begun
headlines.summary: In the coming weeks, readers will increasingly see two rarely used phrases in stories covering our dwindling worldwide oil inventories: "operational minimum" and "tank bottoms." headlines.title: China's LNG Imports Hit Highest Point Since Iran War Began
headlines.summary: China's state-controlled energy giants are buying and importing the highest volumes of liquefied natural gas since the war in Iran began as the world's top LNG importer prepares for peak summer demand and heat waves.
